newsSinceJun6: June 8: GE Aerospace and Wolfspeed signed an MOU to accelerate high-voltage SiC adoption - supply of Wolfspeed's 10 kV MOSFET die plus joint standards for high-voltage power modules targeting solid-state transformers, industrial electrification, aerospace/defense, and faster time-to-power for AI; shares jumped ~13% Monday. June 9: stock fell -13.9%, essentially a full giveback of the MOU pop, attributed to execution-risk concerns around a non-binding MOU with no revenue attached - flow-driven mean reversion, not a fundamental break. Net price vs June 5 is roughly flat with incrementally better on-thesis evidence. Background (June 1-2, pre-window): dedicated Silicon Valley data-center solutions team, new 3.3 kV SiC module families, AI datacenter revenue +50% sequentially off a small base.

sources.label: StreetInsider - GE Aerospace and Wolfspeed sign MOU for silicon carbide development
Yahoo Finance - GE Aerospace and Wolfspeed Collaborate to Accelerate High-Voltage SiC Adoption
Stocktwits - Wolfspeed GE Aerospace deal drives WOLF stock higher
Wolfspeed - Dedicated Data Center Solutions Team launch

verdicts.rankRationale: The GE Aerospace MOU is genuinely new evidence that the SiC strategic optionality this page tracks is real (10 kV die supply, module standards, AI time-to-power framing), and today's -13.9% is a flow-driven giveback that leaves price flat vs June 5 with better evidence - asymmetry modestly improved at rank 98.
verdicts.bull: GE MOU plus the new data-center solutions team and 3.3 kV modules show Wolfspeed converting distressed assets into AI/defense power relevance; at near-bottom rank the optionality is underpriced.
verdicts.neutral: The MOU is non-binding with no revenue commitment; net two-day price action is flat, so the setup is only marginally better than before the announcement.
verdicts.bear: Post-Chapter-11 equity remains fragile - dilution and balance-sheet distress can still overwhelm any strategic optionality, exactly as the page thesis warns.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: MOU converting to a binding supply/standards agreement or a hyperscaler design win from the new data-center team
verdicts.primaryRisk: Further dilution or restructuring fallout wiping out common-equity upside

verdicts.rankRationale: The GE MOU is high-voltage industrial/aero SiC - orthogonal to this page's CPO/HBM/advanced-packaging/test lens - so yesterday's removal call stands; +152% YTD on a $2.3B cap with no page-relevant evidence keeps the asymmetry poor.
verdicts.bull: If AI data-center power architectures (solid-state transformers, HVDC) become a recognized bottleneck adjacent to CPO power delivery, Wolfspeed gains tangential relevance.
verdicts.neutral: News flow is real but off-thesis for this list; the stock round-tripped the MOU pop, leaving the page-specific case unchanged.
verdicts.bear: Zero direct CPO/HBM/packaging exposure plus restructuring-tainted equity makes this the weakest holding on an actionable alpha list - removal remains correct.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Evidence of Wolfspeed SiC content in hyperscaler rack power architectures
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued crowding into an off-thesis name that then unwinds on dilution news

verdicts.rankRationale: GE MOU is directly on this page's thesis - high-voltage SiC for solid-state transformers and faster time-to-power is exactly the AI power-density scaling story - and the -13.9% flow-driven flush improves entry with the new evidence intact.
verdicts.bull: 10 kV MOSFET standardization with GE Aerospace plus 50% sequential AI-datacenter revenue growth validates SiC as a power-density scaling lever before the market prices it.
verdicts.neutral: MOU is non-binding and AI datacenter revenue is off a tiny base; the two-day round-trip shows the market is unwilling to underwrite execution yet.
verdicts.bear: Very-high-risk balance sheet means any macro or SiC-pricing wobble forces dilution, and the +152% YTD already embeds substantial recovery hope.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Binding follow-on to the GE MOU or quantified AI data-center design wins at next earnings
verdicts.primaryRisk: Execution stumble on the new high-voltage roadmap while the post-restructuring capital structure remains stressed

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's call was removal until the post-restructuring dilution/utilization/profitability path clears, and a non-binding MOU with no revenue attached does not resolve any of those questions - no basis to reverse, no new negative either.
verdicts.bull: The GE partnership and data-center team are early proof the power-delivery content thesis can be rebuilt on credible anchor customers.
verdicts.neutral: Net price is flat vs pre-MOU and the balance-sheet questions that drove the removal call are untouched; today's -14% is noise around Monday's pop.
verdicts.bear: A 152% YTD rerating on a restructured equity with unresolved dilution overhang is exactly the post-rerating chase this page penalizes.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Clarity on post-restructuring share count, fab utilization, and a path to positive free cash flow
verdicts.primaryRisk: Equity dilution or covenant stress resurfacing and crushing the recovery premium
